data.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.2 just one month after GPT-5.1's release
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) has launched GPT-5.2, its latest flagship large language model, just one month following the release of GPT-5.1, as the San Francisco-based startup appears determined to stay ahead in the AI race.  

GPT‑5.2 Instant, Thinking and Pro models start rolling out today for ChatGPT users, starting with paid plans. They are all available now in the API for developers. Instant is faster at writing and information seeking; Thinking is designed for structured work like coding and planning; while Pro delivers the most accurate answers for complex questions, according to OpenAI.

"GPT-5.2 is here," said OpenAI CEO Sam Altman in a post on X. "Available today in ChatGPT and the API. It is the smartest generally-available model in the world, and in particular is good at doing real-world knowledge work tasks."

"Already, the average ChatGPT Enterprise user says AI saves them 40–60 minutes a day, and heavy users say it saves them more than 10 hours a week," OpenAI said in a blog post. "We designed GPT‑5.2 to unlock even more economic value for people; it's better at creating spreadsheets, building presentations, writing code, perceiving images, understanding long contexts, using tools, and handling complex, multi-step projects."

GPT-5.2 Thinking significantly surpasses GPT-5.1 Thinking in a wide range of benchmarks, particularly in knowledge work tasks and abstract reasoning. It also demonstrated improvements in software engineering, math and science problems.

"GPT-5.2 represents the biggest leap for GPT models in agentic coding since GPT-5 and is a SOTA coding model in its price range," said Windsurf CEO Jeff Wang. "The version bump undersells the jump in intelligence."

GPT‑5.2 is priced at $1.75/1M input tokens and $14/1M output tokens, with a 90% discount on cached inputs. OpenAI said that despite GPT‑5.2's higher cost per token, the cost of attaining a given level of quality ended up less expensive due to GPT‑5.2's greater token efficiency.

GPT-5.2 was built in collaboration with OpenAI's partners Microsoft (MSFT) and Nvidia (NVDA) utilizing Azure data centers powered by a range of Nvidia's GPUs, including the H100, H200 and GB200-NVL72.

"Super excited about GPT-5.2 from our partners at OpenAI," said Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella in a post on X. "Making it natively part of the Microsoft tools people use every day along with your work data helps all the new model capabilities shine. The pace of progress continues to accelerate."

The release of GPT-5.2 follows recent launches by rivals Anthropic (ANTHRO) and Google (GOOG)(GOOGL), which unveiled Claude Opus 4.5 and Gemini 3, respectively, in November.

data.title: Lovesac outlines fiscal 2026 guidance with $685M–$705M net sales target while focusing on core brand and domestic manufacturing
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Lovesac Company (LOVE) Q3 2026

Management View

  • CEO Shawn Nelson opened by stating that "macro conditions proved a little more challenging than we anticipated with consumer uncertainty leading to meaningful choppiness week-to-week, particularly in our lower dollar volume transactions." He reported third quarter net sales of $150.2 million, which was about $1 million below the guidance range, but highlighted that Lovesac achieved slight year-over-year growth in net sales and market share gains as the category declined.
  • Nelson announced multiple product updates, including the national advertising campaign for Snugg, the launch of the PillowSac Chair Jr., and a new Swept Arm option for Sactionals. He highlighted an upcoming "full redesign of core inserts that will enable domestic manufacturing" for Sactionals, targeting a mid-2026 start for U.S. production.
  • Citing strategic shifts, Nelson said, "We are temporarily slowing the expansion of physical stores in the coming year" and refocusing on core living room offerings, noting, "We will not base our plans on expecting any recovery from the consumer or the category in the near- to medium-term."
  • The company welcomed Jacob Pat as Chief Technology Officer and Wan Ling Martello to the Board of Directors, both bringing digital and global expertise.
  • CFO Keith Siegner stated, "Net sales increased $0.3 million or 0.2% to $150.2 million in the third quarter compared to the prior year period," and explained that "Gross margin decreased 240 basis points to 56.1% of net sales... primarily driven by increases of 320 basis points in inbound transportation and tariff costs, 20 basis points in outbound transportation and warehousing costs, partially offset by an increase of 100 basis points in product margin."

Outlook

  • Siegner projected full year fiscal 2026 net sales of $685 million to $705 million, adjusted EBITDA between $37 million and $43 million, and net income between $2 million and $8 million. Diluted income per common share is estimated at $0.15 to $0.49.
  • For the fourth quarter, guidance is for net sales of $236 million to $256 million, adjusted EBITDA of $51 million to $56 million, net income of $30 million to $36 million, and diluted income per common share of $1.88 to $2.22.
  • The company expects to slow net showroom expansion to approximately 10 net openings in fiscal 2027.

Financial Results

  • Showroom net sales rose by $11.7 million to $102.7 million, while internet net sales fell by $7.6 million to $37.3 million and other net sales dropped by $3.8 million to $10.2 million. Sactional net sales decreased 1.0%, Sacs net sales declined 9.0%, and other net sales—including the new Snugg platform—increased 126.3%.
  • SG&A expense was 49.9% of net sales, up from 47.9%, mainly due to higher payroll, rent, and overhead costs. Advertising and marketing expenses rose to $21.1 million.
  • Operating loss reached $15.8 million, net loss was $10.6 million or (-$0.72) per share, and adjusted EBITDA loss was $6.0 million. The company ended the quarter with $23.7 million in cash and no borrowings.

Q&A

  • Thomas Forte, Maxim Group, asked about discount levels and gross margin for the Loved by Lovesac recommerce efforts. Mary Fox, President & COO, responded that discounts are "around about a 20% to 25% discount level to what you'd typically be able to receive if you were buying at full price or at a discount level," and Siegner added that "this year has largely been about building the infrastructure and capabilities to get the program to a broad base of folks."
  • Michael Baker, D.A. Davidson, inquired about the P&L impact of strategic shifts for fiscal 2027. Siegner said, "We're really in the midst of landing the fourth quarter and ending up this fiscal year and in the process of our formal AOP planning for fiscal '27 at this point. Give us a couple of months, and we'll give you a lot more details."
  • Baker followed up on the cautious Q4 outlook. Nelson replied, "It's an abundance of caution. We have tough compares coming over the new year. Back to the industry, for November, it was down 3%, but the high end was down 11%, just to make that real for you, right?"
  • Eric Des Lauriers, Craig-Hallum, asked about revenue weakness and marketing shifts. Fox said the softness was "primarily the small setup Sactionals" and highlighted "encouraging signs as the quarter progressed" due to marketing changes.
  • Matt Koranda, ROTH Capital, asked if comps were positive quarter-to-date. Fox replied, "Yes, the comps are positive for this quarter, and we actually had a strong start to the quarter that has continued all the way through to today."
  • Koranda also questioned gross margin outlook. Siegner clarified, "It's the incremental need for a step-up in promotions to remain competitive, particularly as we target that below $6,000 transaction as well as some deleverage against fixed costs."
  • Brian Nagel, Oppenheimer, discussed the benefits of domestic manufacturing. Nelson emphasized, "This will have ideally a positive impact on our P&L. But then there's the warehousing and inventory carry impact as well, you can only imagine."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts presented a tone of cautious optimism blended with concern, particularly regarding guidance cuts, margin pressures, and the P&L effects of strategic pivots. Repeated requests for clarity on margins and growth plans indicated skepticism about the near-term outlook.
  • Management maintained a pragmatic and measured tone, often reiterating themes of caution—"out of an abundance of caution"—and prudence, especially in reference to guidance and promotional strategy. During prepared remarks, the tone was more confident and forward-looking, citing product innovation and operational resilience; in Q&A, responses were more defensive and explanatory.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone shifted from moderate optimism with a focus on innovation to increased caution, especially around macro challenges and consumer demand, while analysts’ inquiries became more pointed on issues of margin and growth sustainability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for fiscal 2026 net sales was revised to $685 million–$705 million from the previous range of $710 million–$740 million, reflecting a more conservative outlook. Adjusted EBITDA guidance also decreased.
  • Gross margin challenges persisted, with further pressure from tariffs and increased promotional activity; margins slipped to 56.1% from 56.4% in Q2.
  • The strategic focus has shifted more distinctly toward harvesting existing brand equity, slowing physical store expansion, and accelerating domestic manufacturing initiatives. Product innovation announcements grew more robust, with a high volume of launches planned for fiscal 2027.
  • Analysts’ focus moved from marketing effectiveness and brand evolution to more pointed questions on financial impacts and margin sustainability. Management’s confidence in innovation remained, but caution increased regarding macro headwinds.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty, consumer price sensitivity, and heightened promotional pressure as primary challenges. The impact of tariffs and transportation costs on gross margin remains significant.
  • The company is mitigating these risks through price increases, cost savings, vendor concessions, and a shift toward more domestic production. Marketing strategies have shifted to digital and influencer-led campaigns to drive conversion.
  • Analysts voiced concerns over the sustainability of margins, the P&L impact of strategic changes, and the prudence of slowing showroom expansion.

Final Takeaway

Lovesac’s leadership expressed determination to navigate ongoing macroeconomic headwinds by focusing on core brand strength, accelerating innovation in its Designed for Life platforms, and launching new domestic manufacturing for key products. Fiscal 2026 guidance reflects a more cautious approach, with an emphasis on market share gains and profitability through efficiency, channel optimization, and a robust pipeline of product launches for the coming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Standard Chartered upgraded to Buy at Goldman on potential for guidance boost
data.description:
Goldman Sachs upgraded Standard Chartered Plc (SCBFF) to Buy from Neutral on the potential for management to boost its guidance after its 2025 earnings are released or at its investor day in May.

The upgrade "is especially driven by our increased conviction in the bank's sustained improving profitability outlook, where we forecast underlying ROTEs to reach 14.6% by the end of 2025E (exceeding management's guidance of 13% ROTE) and to rise further to 15.0% by 2027E and 15.7% by 2028E," analyst Melissa Kuang wrote in a note to clients.

Even after the stock's 86% rally year-to-date, Goldman sees room for further re-rating as its current valuation of 1.2 P/TB on the Hong Kong exchange is compelling compared with its peers.

"The bank is demonstrating strong execution across revenue and cost lines to deliver improving profitability," Kuang said. The trajectory is driven by robust momentum in the affluent sector, steady market share gains in global markets and banking, and potential for increased operational leverage, she added.

data.title: Abbott asks Congress to shield it baby formula lawsuits - report
data.description:
• Abbott Laboratories (ABT) is now lobbying lawmakers to protect it from numerous lawsuits that argue its infant formulas can cause a severe intestinal disease in babies as well as be shielded from potential future legal actions. 
  • The company is also considering transferring sales and distribution control of its formulas to the federal government, Bloomberg reported. 
  • Claims against Abbott suggest that the company failed to warn that its infant formulas, such as Similac, could increase the likelihood of developing necrotizing enterocolitis, a severe inflammation of the intestinal tissue in premature babies. 
  • An Abbott spokesperson told Bloomberg “Numerous studies and NEC authorities have made clear that preterm infant formula does not cause NEC."
  • Abbott has spent millions of dollars on its lobbying effort, the news service reported, citing disclosure documents and federal records. 
  • Baby formula maker baby formula maker Mead Johnson, owned by Reckitt Benckiser (RBGPF)(RBGLY), is also facing NEC lawsuits.
  • Separately, in 2022, Abbott initiated a major recall of its baby formula following reports of bacterial contamination at one of its Michigan plants.

data.title: Luminar Technologies jumps after Rivian talks up LiDAR potential at its Autonomy and AI Day event
data.description:
Luminar Technologies (LAZR) shot up in Thursday afternoon trading after the company was mentioned at Rivian Automotive's (RIVN) Autonomy and AI Day. 

Rivian Automotive (RIVN) said it plans to integrate LiDAR into future R2 models. The electric vehicle maker said LiDAR will augment the company’s multi-modal sensor strategy, providing detailed, three-dimensional spatial data and redundant sensing, and improving real-time detection for the edge cases of driving.

In addition, Rivian Automotive (RIVN) highlighted that its Gen 3 autonomy hardware, including ACM3 and LiDAR, is currently undergoing validation and is expected to start shipping on R2 models starting at the end of 2026.

Shares of Luminar Technologies (LAZR) were up 6.8% at 1:35 p.m. Short interest on LAZR stands at 28.5% of the total float.

data.title: Short interest in S&P 500 industrial sector stocks jumped significantly in November
data.description:
Short-selling activity increased notably in S&P 500 industrial stocks during November, coinciding with a decline in the sector’s performance.

By the end of the November month, the average short interest across the S&P 500 industrial sector climbed to 2.49%, an 18-basis-point rise from 2.31% in October.

The S&P 500 Industrial ETF (XLI) slipped 0.88% in November, even as the broader S&P 500 edged up 0.13% over the same period.

Short interest, which could be an indicator of pessimism, calculates the number of shares sold against the company float. (Short Interest % = Number of Shares Sold Short ÷ Stock Float).

Most shorted stocks:

Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL) - 7.73% vs 6.02% last month.

Generac Holdings (GNRC) - 7.05% vs 6.08%.

Southwest Airlines (LUV) - 6.28% vs 6.56%.

C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) - 6.27% vs 6.33%. 

Lennox International (LII) - 5.59% vs 4.95%.

Least shorted stocks:

RTX (RTX) - 0.82% vs 0.93% last month.

General Dynamics (GD) - 0.87% vs 0.85%.

AMETEK (AME) - 1.03% vs 0.97%.

Parker-Hannifin (PH) - 1.06% vs 1.15%.

Northrop Grumman (NOC) - 1.17% vs 0.84%.

Industry Analysis:

 

Short sellers increased their positioning across most corners of the Industrial sector, with the sharpest activity concentrated in transportation, machinery, engineering, and industrial software, while passenger airlines (SP500-20302010) continue to draw the highest bearish interest in the group.

 

Passenger Airlines continued to be the most heavily shorted industry in the industrial sector, with short interest rising to 4.39% in November from 4.29% in October. Software ranked second, with short interest climbing to 3.92% from 3.28% the month prior. At the other end of the spectrum, Environmental and Facilities Services (SP500-20201050) remained the least shorted industry, holding a 1.48% short-interest level at November’s close.

data.title: As the S&P 500 hovers near record highs, see the 10 most overbought stocks in the index
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) has recently jumped up close to all-time trading highs, pushing several constituent stocks into overbought territory.

Based on the relative strength index, a widely used technical indicator, 68 of the S&P 500 stocks are currently classified as overbought, while only eight remain in oversold territory. 

For reference, an RSI above 70 typically signals overbought conditions, suggesting potential short-term pullbacks, whereas a reading below 30 indicates oversold levels, often highlighting potential buying opportunities. 

Below is a list of the 10 most oversold S&P 500 stocks, according to the latest RSI readings.

No. 1: EPAM Systems (EPAM), RSI of 86.23.

No. 2: Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), RSI of 85.15.

No. 3: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, RSI of 83.35.

No. 4: Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY), RSI of 81.49.

No. 5: U.S. Bancorp (USB), RSI of 81.26.

No. 6: Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH), RSI of 81.24.

No. 7: General Motors (GM), RSI of 80.99.

No. 8: Fox Corporation (FOXA), RSI of 80.85.

No. 9: Southwest Airlines (LUV), RSI of 80.62.

No. 10: Truist Financial Corporation (TFC), RSI of 80.44.

S&P 500 Funds: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX).

data.title: Market Voices: Lilly's new obesity drug, Bessent, EQT's robotics move
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact markets, sectors, or individual stocks.

  • Eli Lilly (LLY) said that a late-stage study showed its new injected medication retatrutide helped obese patients lose up to 24% of their body weight in 68 weeks, with an average reduction in knee pain of around 63% at the same dosage level. 

The results exceeded expectations. Analysts had been expecting average weight loss of around 20% to 23%, with at least a 50% reduction in knee pain, according to Bloomberg.

Lilly has been testing the drug for the treatment of overweight and obese patients with knee osteoarthritis. 

"We are encouraged by the results of TRIUMPH-4, which highlight the powerful effect of retatrutide, a first-in-class triple agonist, on body weight, pain and physical function," said Kenneth Custer, president of Lilly's cardiometabolic health unit, in a statement. 

"With seven additional Phase 3 readouts expected in 2026, we believe retatrutide could become an important option for patients with significant weight loss needs and certain complications, including knee osteoarthritis," Custer added.

Lilly markets the popular weight-loss drug Zepbound, also known as Mounjaro, which competes with Novo Nordisk's (NVO) Wegovy and Ozempic. 

  • Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has proposed changing the focus of the Financial Stability Oversight Council to one of deregulation rather than increased regulation.

According to CNBC, Bessent, who chairs the council, is set to release a letter that states, “The Council will work with and support member agencies in considering whether aspects of the U.S. financial regulatory framework impose undue burdens and negatively impact economic growth, thereby undermining financial stability.”

The FSOC was established in the aftermath of the 2008 financial crisis to monitor and address systemic risks in the financial sector, CNBC noted. 

  • Swedish private equity group EQT (EQBBF) is planning to distribute 10,000 humanoid robots developed by one of its startup investments, 1X Technologies, to other companies in its portfolio to help with tasks in such sectors as manufacturing and nursing home care. 

Under the plan, the robots will be leased or purchased by the companies, with deployments expected to begin next year. Financial terms were not disclosed, according to Bloomberg.

“This can provide companies with a real unfair advantage to get their hands on it early,” EQT Partner Ted Persson told Bloomberg.

1X Technologies also counts OpenAI (OPENAI) and Tiger Global as investors, Bloomberg noted.

Other companies engaged in developing humanoid robots include Tesla (TSLA) and Hyundai's Boston Dynamics.

data.title: Target climbs for a seventh session
data.description:
Target (TGT) shares rose 2.48% to $96.97 in the afternoon trade on Thursday, adding to a seventh consecutive session of gain.

Target has been drawing interest during the holiday period as it tests overnight delivery using sorting centers and gig drivers to better compete with Amazon and Walmart. The retailer has also seen solid Black Friday traffic and benefited from record Cyber Monday spending as heavy discounting and AI-driven online shopping lifted demand for electronics and toys.

Still, analysts remain cautious on Target’s ability to attract new customers, and the company is navigating corporate job cuts tied to its broader efficiency push.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, Target is a Hold with a score of 3.08 out of 5, supported by grades of A in profitability, B+ in valuation, but weighed down by weaker grades of C+ in terms of both momentum and revisions and F in growth. SA analysts, however, suggest buying the stock.

Seeking Alpha analyst Alan Galecki said Target’s weak free cash flow, rising capex and soft discretionary sales threaten the company’s long dividend streak, adding that “cheap can always get cheaper when the payout is at risk.”

Wall Street sentiment is more cautious with 10 out of 38 analysts rating the shares a Buy or higher, 23 recommend Hold, four recommending a Sell, and one suggests a Strong Sell.

The stock has climbed 5.58% in the past month, though it remains 28.61% down YTD.
